text: Numerous scientific disciplines have achieved significant breakthroughs by adopting online platforms that organize, structure, and mine complex data typically too granular for traditional journal publication. In this paper, we introduce OpenML, an open-science platform designed for machine learning researchers to share and organize their research artifacts in exhaustive detail. By streamlining the exchange of data and results, OpenML enables researchers to work more efficiently, increase their professional visibility, and collaborate globally to tackle increasingly complex challenges. We explore how OpenML fits into the broader landscape of networked science and outline the tangible benefits it offers to the machine learning community, including researchers, students, and industry practitioners.
